occupations.title: Sales Assistants (General)
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sales Assistants require physical presence in retail environments to handle goods, stock shelves, and provide face-to-face customer service. While AI and automation, such as smart inventory systems and advanced self-checkouts, will streamline operations and may reduce overall headcount, the core physical and interpersonal aspects of the role provide a strong barrier to direct AI replacement.

occupations.title: Aged and Disabled Carers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Aged and Disabled Carers perform highly physical and interpersonal work, providing essential in-home support and companionship that AI cannot replicate. While AI might streamline scheduling or case note documentation, the core tasks require human empathy, physical presence, and manual dexterity. In the Australian context, driven by the NDIS and an aging population, demand for these human-centric roles will remain extremely high and insulated from digital automation.

occupations.title: Registered Nurses
occupations.exposure_rationale: Registered nursing is fundamentally a hands-on, interpersonal profession requiring physical presence and human empathy, which limits direct AI automation. However, AI will increasingly assist with administrative tasks, patient monitoring, and diagnostic support, leading to a moderate level of exposure.

occupations.title: General Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: General Clerks primarily handle routine administrative and data processing tasks, which are highly susceptible to automation through current AI and robotic process automation technologies. Because the role is almost entirely computer-based and involves repetitive digital workflows, AI tools can easily streamline or replace these functions in the Australian workforce.

occupations.title: Retail Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Retail Managers require physical presence to oversee store operations, manage staff, and handle customer interactions, which limits direct AI replacement. However, AI will significantly streamline back-office tasks like inventory forecasting, staff rostering, and sales reporting. In the Australian retail sector, this will likely shift the role away from administration towards pure floor management and customer experience.

occupations.title: Accountants
occupations.exposure_rationale: Accounting is predominantly computer-based knowledge work involving data analysis, reporting, and compliance. AI tools are rapidly advancing in their ability to automate routine financial processing, tax preparation, and auditing tasks. While strategic advisory roles will remain, the core analytical and reporting functions are highly exposed to AI-driven productivity gains.

occupations.title: Electricians
occupations.exposure_rationale: Electricians perform highly physical, hands-on work in unpredictable environments like construction sites and domestic properties, which AI cannot replicate. While AI might streamline minor administrative tasks like scheduling, invoicing, or accessing schematics, the core tasks of installing and repairing electrical systems require manual dexterity and physical presence. In the Australian context, strong demand and severe labour shortages in trades further insulate this role from AI-driven displacement.

occupations.title: Software and Applications Programmers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Software programming is entirely computer-based and directly targeted by current generative AI capabilities like coding assistants. While AI will significantly boost productivity and reshape how developers work rather than completely replacing them, the automation of routine coding, testing, and documentation tasks leads to very high exposure.

occupations.title: Truck Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Truck driving is an inherently physical occupation requiring real-world navigation and vehicle operation across Australia's vast transport networks. While AI will optimize logistics, route planning, and dispatching, current digital AI cannot replace the physical task of driving, and autonomous trucking remains a future robotics challenge rather than a near-term digital AI exposure.

occupations.title: Receptionists
occupations.exposure_rationale: While the physical greeting aspect of a receptionist provides some barrier to full automation, the core tasks of answering inquiries, scheduling, and processing information are highly exposed to AI voice agents and chatbots. In the Australian context, widespread adoption of automated check-ins and AI-driven booking systems in medical, corporate, and hospitality sectors will significantly reduce the number of receptionists required.

occupations.title: Child Carers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Childcare is a highly physical and interpersonal occupation requiring constant supervision, emotional intelligence, and hands-on care for children. While AI may assist with minor administrative duties like drafting parent updates or managing schedules, the core responsibilities cannot be automated. In the Australian context, this remains a high-demand, low-AI-exposure role due to strict safety ratios and the essential human element of early childhood development.

occupations.title: Primary School Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI will significantly streamline lesson planning, grading, and administrative tasks, the core of primary school teaching relies heavily on interpersonal skills, emotional intelligence, and physical classroom management. In the Australian context, where there is a critical shortage of teachers, AI will act as a productivity tool rather than a replacement, keeping overall exposure moderate.

occupations.title: Advertising, Public Relations and Sales Manager
occupations.exposure_rationale: Advertising, PR, and Sales Managers rely heavily on data analysis, content creation, and market research, all of which are highly exposed to generative AI and predictive analytics. However, the core of the role involves strategic decision-making, team leadership, and relationship building, which require human judgment and interpersonal skills, preventing full automation.

occupations.title: Storepersons
occupations.exposure_rationale: Storepersons perform highly physical tasks involving the receipt, handling, and dispatch of goods in warehouses. While AI will increasingly optimize inventory management systems and logistics planning, current digital AI cannot replace the physical labor required to move and handle stock, keeping direct exposure low.

occupations.title: Contract, Program and Project Administrators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Contract and project administration involves heavy document processing, scheduling, and reporting, which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While stakeholder communication requires a human touch, AI tools will significantly increase productivity, meaning fewer administrators will be needed to manage the same number of projects in Australia's corporate and government sectors.

occupations.title: Secondary School Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI can significantly assist Secondary School Teachers with lesson planning, grading, and administrative tasks, the core of the role involves interpersonal engagement, classroom management, and fostering students' emotional and social development. These physical and deeply human elements provide a strong barrier to full automation, keeping overall AI exposure moderate.

occupations.title: Kitchenhands
occupations.exposure_rationale: Kitchenhand roles are almost entirely physical, involving manual tasks like cleaning, basic food preparation, and assisting service staff in fast-paced hospitality environments. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate these physical, real-world tasks. Consequently, this occupation has minimal exposure to AI disruption in the Australian labour market.

occupations.title: Commercial Cleaners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Commercial cleaning is an entirely physical occupation requiring workers to navigate unpredictable real-world environments like offices, hospitals, and construction sites. Current digital AI capabilities cannot perform manual cleaning tasks, and the role involves virtually no computer-based knowledge work. While AI might slightly optimize scheduling or dispatch, the core job remains highly insulated from digital AI disruption.

occupations.title: Carpenters and Joiners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical trade requiring manual dexterity, spatial problem-solving, and on-site presence in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like quoting or scheduling, the core work of cutting, shaping, and installing materials cannot be automated by current digital AI. In the Australian context, where construction trades face significant shortages, demand for these physical skills remains highly insulated from AI disruption.

occupations.title: Accounting Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Accounting clerks perform highly routine, computer-based tasks such as monitoring accounts and processing financial documentation. These tasks are highly susceptible to automation through AI-driven accounting software, document extraction, and automated data entry. In the Australian context, this represents a significant area for efficiency gains, leading to very high AI exposure.

occupations.title: Education Aides
occupations.exposure_rationale: Education Aides require physical presence in classrooms to supervise, care for, and assist students, making the core of their work highly interpersonal and hands-on. While AI might help with some minor administrative tasks or material preparation, the aide's essential role in direct student support and behavioural management cannot be automated.

occupations.title: Office Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Office Managers handle administrative systems, scheduling, and personnel coordination, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While managing physical office resources and interpersonal staff relations provide some buffer, the core administrative and organizational tasks can be heavily streamlined by AI tools, significantly reducing the time required for these duties.

occupations.title: Construction Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Construction Managers oversee complex, physical building sites and manage diverse teams of tradespeople, which inherently limits full AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance project scheduling, budgeting, and supply chain logistics, the role requires on-site presence, real-world problem solving, and interpersonal negotiation in the Australian construction sector.

occupations.title: Chefs
occupations.exposure_rationale: Chefs operate in highly physical, fast-paced environments requiring manual dexterity, sensory judgment, and real-time coordination. While AI can assist with administrative tasks like menu planning, inventory management, and cost analysis, the core physical act of cooking remains entirely insulated from digital AI. In the Australian hospitality sector, the demand for human chefs will remain robust despite minor administrative efficiencies.

occupations.title: Waiters
occupations.exposure_rationale: Waiters perform highly physical and interpersonal tasks, such as carrying food, navigating dining rooms, and interacting directly with patrons. While digital ordering systems and AI-driven management tools may streamline the ordering process, the core physical service cannot be automated by current digital AI, keeping exposure low in the Australian hospitality sector.

occupations.title: Purchasing and Supply Logistics Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Purchasing and supply logistics clerks primarily perform routine digital tasks such as processing orders, monitoring inventory, and scheduling. These data-heavy, administrative functions are highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven supply chain management tools, leading to very high AI exposure.

occupations.title: Bar Attendants and Baristas
occupations.exposure_rationale: The role of a bar attendant or barista is highly physical and relies heavily on manual dexterity and face-to-face customer service within Australia's large hospitality sector. Current digital AI cannot physically prepare beverages or replicate the human element of hospitality, limiting AI's impact to minor backend tasks like inventory forecasting or point-of-sale automation.

occupations.title: Checkout Operators and Office Cashiers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While checkout operators require physical presence to handle goods and interact with customers, their core transactional tasks are highly routine. In the Australian retail sector, AI-driven computer vision and advanced self-checkout technologies are rapidly accelerating the automation of these roles, leading to a projected decline in employment.

occupations.title: Metal Fitters and Machinists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Metal fitters and machinists perform highly physical tasks involving the assembly, shaping, and maintenance of metal parts and machinery. While AI may assist in generating CNC programs or predictive maintenance schedules, the core work requires hands-on mechanical skills and physical presence in workshops or industrial sites, providing a strong barrier to digital AI disruption.

occupations.title: Nursing Support and Personal Care Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Nursing support and personal care work is fundamentally physical and interpersonal, requiring direct human contact, empathy, and manual handling in healthcare settings. While AI may streamline some administrative or scheduling tasks, the core responsibilities of patient care cannot be automated by current digital AI. This aligns with the low AI exposure typical of Australia's growing aged care and healthcare support sectors.

occupations.title: Motor Mechanics
occupations.exposure_rationale: Motor mechanics perform highly physical and manual tasks involving the repair and maintenance of vehicles, which AI cannot replicate. While AI and advanced software will increasingly assist with vehicle diagnostics and workshop administration, the core mechanical work requires hands-on physical presence and dexterity.

occupations.title: Management and Organisation Analysts
occupations.exposure_rationale: Management and Organisation Analysts primarily perform knowledge work, including data analysis, process mapping, and report writing, which are highly exposed to AI automation and augmentation. However, the role also requires significant stakeholder management, complex problem-solving, and navigating ambiguous organisational dynamics, which provides some buffer against full automation. In the Australian corporate and government sectors, AI will likely significantly boost productivity in this role, reshaping how analysts work.

occupations.title: Real Estate Sales Agents
occupations.exposure_rationale: AI will significantly streamline the administrative side of real estate, such as drafting marketing copy, analyzing market trends, and managing routine lease inquiries. However, the core of the role relies heavily on physical property inspections, face-to-face negotiations, and building interpersonal trust, which limits full automation.

occupations.title: Plumbers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Plumbing is a highly physical trade requiring manual dexterity and problem-solving in unpredictable, real-world environments across Australian construction and residential sectors. While AI may assist with minor administrative tasks like scheduling or invoicing, the core work of installing and repairing physical infrastructure is entirely immune to current digital AI capabilities.

occupations.title: Solicitors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Solicitors primarily engage in text-heavy knowledge work, such as drafting legal documents, reviewing contracts, and conducting legal research, which are highly susceptible to automation and augmentation by current large language models. While AI will significantly boost productivity in these areas, the need for nuanced client negotiations, strategic counsel, and human judgment in the Australian legal system prevents total automation.

occupations.title: Advertising and Marketing Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Advertising and marketing are heavily computer-based knowledge professions highly exposed to generative AI capabilities like copywriting, image generation, and market data analysis. While strategic client relationship management remains human-centric, AI tools will significantly automate content creation and campaign optimization. This will likely drive massive productivity gains, reducing the need for junior roles in Australian agencies and corporate marketing departments.

occupations.title: ICT Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: ICT Managers operate entirely in the digital and knowledge economy, making their core tasks of system planning, data analysis, and technical strategy highly exposed to AI tools. However, their strong focus on leadership, vendor negotiation, and organisational strategy provides a buffer against full automation. In the Australian corporate and government sectors, AI will heavily augment their decision-making and project management capabilities rather than replace the managerial function itself.

occupations.title: Welfare Support Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Welfare Support Workers rely heavily on emotional intelligence, empathy, and interpersonal communication to assist vulnerable individuals, which AI cannot replicate. However, AI will significantly streamline the administrative aspects of the role, such as case management, reporting, and service coordination. Overall exposure is moderate; AI will act as a powerful assistant for bureaucratic tasks but will not replace the core human-to-human support required in the Australian community services sector.

occupations.title: Other Hospitality, Retail and Service Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: These management roles involve overseeing physical facilities, staff, and customer service operations, which require significant on-site presence and interpersonal skills. While AI will streamline administrative tasks like rostering, inventory management, and reporting, the core responsibilities of managing physical environments and human interactions limit overall exposure.

occupations.title: Human Resource Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Human Resource Managers perform predominantly knowledge-based work that is highly exposed to AI tools capable of automating recruitment screening, policy drafting, and workforce analytics. While navigating complex Australian workplace relations (such as modern awards and enterprise agreements) and handling sensitive interpersonal conflict resolution require human judgment, AI will significantly enhance productivity and reshape the administrative and analytical components of the role.

occupations.title: General Practitioners and Resident Medical Officers
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI will significantly streamline clinical documentation, medical record analysis, and diagnostic support, the core of a General Practitioner's role relies on physical examinations, empathetic patient communication, and complex human judgment. In the Australian healthcare system, which currently faces severe GP shortages, AI will act as a powerful productivity tool rather than a replacement, keeping overall exposure moderate.

occupations.title: Bookkeepers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Bookkeeping consists almost entirely of routine digital processing, data entry, and financial reconciliation, which are highly susceptible to automation. Advanced AI integrations within popular Australian accounting platforms like Xero and MYOB can already autonomously extract invoice data, categorize transactions, and perform reconciliations, significantly reducing the need for human intervention.

occupations.title: Gardeners
occupations.exposure_rationale: Gardeners perform highly physical, outdoor work involving planting, maintenance, and landscaping, which current digital AI cannot automate. While AI tools may assist with minor tasks like landscape design planning or diagnosing plant diseases via image recognition, the core execution remains entirely manual and requires physical presence.

occupations.title: Human Resource Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Human Resource Professionals face high AI exposure as generative AI can efficiently handle recruitment screening, policy drafting, and routine employee inquiries. However, their role in navigating Australia's complex Fair Work regulations, enterprise bargaining, and sensitive workplace dispute resolution requires high emotional intelligence and interpersonal skills, preventing full automation.

occupations.title: Information Officers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Information Officers primarily handle routine inquiries, complaints, and information provision, tasks that are highly susceptible to automation via advanced conversational AI and chatbots. While some face-to-face roles exist, the bulk of this work is digital or phone-based, making it one of the most exposed occupations to current AI capabilities in Australia.

occupations.title: Delivery Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Delivery driving is an inherently physical occupation requiring navigation of unpredictable real-world environments and manual handling of goods. While AI will continue to optimize routing and logistics software, current digital AI capabilities cannot automate the physical delivery process, keeping exposure minimal.

occupations.title: Structural Steel and Welding Trades Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Structural steel and welding work is inherently physical, requiring manual dexterity, spatial awareness, and on-site presence in construction, mining, or manufacturing environments. Current digital AI capabilities cannot perform the physical tasks of cutting, shaping, and joining metal. While AI might assist slightly with blueprint analysis or project administration, the core trade remains highly insulated from digital automation.

occupations.title: Architectural, Building and Surveying Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: While site inspections and physical surveying require on-site presence, a significant portion of the role involves data evaluation, cost estimation, and drafting plans. AI tools integrated into CAD, BIM, and surveying software will increasingly automate these digital tasks, leading to moderate overall exposure.

occupations.title: Finance Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Finance Managers perform highly analytical and computer-based work, making core tasks like financial reporting, forecasting, and data analysis highly exposed to AI automation. However, their role also involves strategic decision-making, stakeholder management, and team leadership, which require human judgment and interpersonal skills, keeping their exposure from being absolute.

occupations.title: Early Childhood (Pre-primary School) Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Early childhood teaching is fundamentally interpersonal and requires physical presence to manage, care for, and educate young children. While AI can assist with lesson planning and administrative reporting, the core tasks of emotional support, behavioural management, and hands-on learning cannot be automated. In Australia's high-demand early education sector, AI will serve as a minor administrative aid rather than a replacement.

occupations.title: Civil Engineering Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Civil engineering involves significant complex knowledge work, such as structural analysis, cost estimation, and design, which AI can highly augment. However, the role also requires physical site oversight, dealing with real-world unpredictability, and strict regulatory sign-offs in the Australian construction sector, which limits full automation.

occupations.title: Police
occupations.exposure_rationale: While AI will significantly streamline administrative tasks, report writing, and investigative data analysis for Australian police forces, the core duties require physical presence, complex human judgment, and real-world intervention. The unpredictable nature of law enforcement and the need for interpersonal community engagement provide a strong barrier to full automation, resulting in moderate overall exposure.

occupations.title: ICT Support Technicians
occupations.exposure_rationale: ICT Support Technicians face very high AI exposure as advanced language models and AI diagnostic tools can automate routine troubleshooting, ticketing, and Level 1/2 support queries. While the physical deployment and maintenance of hardware infrastructure in Australian offices and data centres provide some protection, the vast majority of software and network support tasks will be heavily streamlined by AI.

occupations.title: Other Miscellaneous Labourers
occupations.exposure_rationale: This category comprises highly physical, manual, and outdoor roles such as traffic controllers, trades assistants, and trolley collectors. Current digital AI capabilities cannot perform these physical tasks in unpredictable real-world environments. While some roles like ticket collection may face traditional automation, overall generative AI exposure is minimal.

occupations.title: Database and Systems Administrators, and ICT Security Specialists
occupations.exposure_rationale: This occupation is entirely computer-based, making it highly exposed to AI advancements in code generation, automated system monitoring, and threat detection. While AI will significantly boost productivity and automate routine administration and log analysis, human oversight remains critical for complex system architecture and strategic security planning in Australian enterprises.

occupations.title: Shelf Fillers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Shelf filling is an entirely physical occupation requiring manual labour in Australian supermarkets and retail stores. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate the physical manipulation of goods onto shelves, as this would require advanced robotics rather than generative AI. Therefore, the exposure to digital AI is minimal.

occupations.title: Building and Plumbing Labourers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Building and plumbing labourers perform highly physical tasks in unpredictable, real-world environments such as construction sites and trenches. Current digital AI capabilities cannot automate manual labour, excavation, or pipe installation. While AI may optimise construction scheduling at a management level, the core physical duties of these labourers remain entirely insulated from AI disruption.

occupations.title: Sales Representatives
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sales Representatives spend significant time on lead generation, CRM management, and drafting communications, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, B2B sales still rely heavily on human relationship building, negotiation, and trust, which prevents full automation but will likely reduce the total number of reps needed.

occupations.title: Other Specialist Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Specialist managers in fields like arts, environment, and sports engage heavily in knowledge work, administration, and strategic planning, which AI can significantly streamline through automated reporting and data analysis. However, their roles also require complex stakeholder management, leadership, and occasional physical oversight, providing a buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: Livestock Farmers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Livestock farming is a highly physical occupation set in unpredictable outdoor environments, making direct automation by digital AI nearly impossible. While AI can assist with farm management, weather forecasting, and market analysis, the core tasks of breeding, raising, and handling livestock require physical presence and manual intervention.

occupations.title: Forklift Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Forklift driving is an entirely physical occupation requiring spatial awareness and manual operation of machinery in warehouses and industrial sites. While AI may optimize warehouse logistics and inventory systems, current digital AI capabilities cannot perform the physical task of moving goods. Although autonomous robotics are emerging in large Australian logistics hubs, the direct exposure to digital AI over the next 5 years remains minimal.

occupations.title: Hairdressers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Hairdressing is a highly physical, hands-on profession requiring fine motor skills, spatial awareness, and direct interpersonal interaction with clients. While AI may assist with salon booking systems or virtual hairstyle previews, the core tasks of cutting, colouring, and styling hair cannot be automated by current digital AI.

occupations.title: Sports Coaches, Instructors and Officials
occupations.exposure_rationale: Sports coaching and officiating are inherently physical and interpersonal roles requiring real-time observation, motivation, and physical presence. While AI can assist with generating training programs or analyzing performance data, the core tasks of instructing athletes and managing sporting events cannot be automated. Therefore, AI exposure is low and limited to supplementary administrative or analytical tasks.

occupations.title: General Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: General Managers rely heavily on complex decision-making, leadership, and interpersonal negotiation, which are highly resistant to AI automation. However, AI will significantly reshape their roles by automating data analysis, performance tracking, and report generation, augmenting their productivity rather than replacing the need for human accountability.

occupations.title: Drillers, Miners and Shot Firers
occupations.exposure_rationale: This occupation is highly physical and takes place in unpredictable, real-world environments such as Australian mine sites and demolition zones. While the mining sector uses some physical automation, current digital AI capabilities cannot perform the manual tasks of assembling rigs, operating heavy plant, or handling explosives. AI exposure is minimal, limited mostly to background geological analysis or safety reporting.

occupations.title: Security Officers and Guards
occupations.exposure_rationale: Security officers require physical presence for deterrence, patrolling, and managing unpredictable real-world situations. While AI will enhance surveillance systems through computer vision and automated alerts, the core role of physical intervention and on-site security remains largely unaffected by generative AI.

occupations.title: Production Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Production managers in Australia's critical mining, forestry, and manufacturing sectors spend significant time overseeing physical operations and managing human teams on-site. While AI will heavily augment their planning, scheduling, and predictive maintenance tasks, the core responsibilities of safety management, physical resource coordination, and personnel leadership provide a strong barrier to full automation.

occupations.title: Financial Investment Advisers and Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Financial investment advising involves significant data analysis, market research, and report generation, which are highly exposed to AI automation. However, the role also requires building client trust, navigating complex Australian financial regulations, and understanding nuanced personal circumstances, which provides some buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: University Lecturers and Tutors
occupations.exposure_rationale: University lecturers and tutors are highly exposed to AI, as core tasks like course preparation, grading, literature reviews, and data analysis can be significantly automated or augmented by generative AI. While the interpersonal aspects of teaching, mentoring, and physical lab research provide some buffer, the heavy reliance on digital knowledge processing means AI will deeply reshape academic workloads in Australia's higher education sector.

occupations.title: Cafe and Restaurant Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Cafe and Restaurant Managers require a strong physical presence to oversee daily operations, manage staff, and interact with customers in dynamic environments. While AI can streamline administrative tasks like inventory forecasting, staff rostering, and financial reporting, the core responsibilities of customer service and real-time problem solving remain highly insulated from automation.

occupations.title: Chief Executives and Managing Directors
occupations.exposure_rationale: While Chief Executives rely heavily on data and reports that AI will increasingly generate and analyze, the core of their role involves high-level strategic decision-making, leadership, and stakeholder management. AI cannot replace the human accountability, complex negotiation, and interpersonal skills required at the executive level. Therefore, AI will act as a powerful decision-support tool rather than a force for automation or job reduction in this occupation.

occupations.title: Graphic and Web Designers, and Illustrators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Graphic and web design is almost entirely computer-based and heavily exposed to generative AI tools capable of producing images, layouts, and code. In Australia's creative and tech sectors, AI will significantly automate routine design tasks and boost productivity, meaning fewer designers may be needed for standard commercial work.

occupations.title: Supply, Distribution and Procurement Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Supply and procurement management involves significant data analysis, forecasting, and contract management, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role oversees physical supply chains, the day-to-day work is predominantly computer-based knowledge work. However, strategic decision-making and vendor negotiations provide some buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: Other Information and Organisation Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: These roles involve heavy document analysis, policy interpretation, and written communication, which are highly exposed to generative AI capabilities. While interpersonal duties in liaison and electorate roles provide some buffer, the core information processing tasks will be significantly reshaped by AI.

occupations.title: Packers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Packers perform highly physical and manual tasks such as weighing, wrapping, and sealing physical goods. While physical automation and robotics are transforming Australian logistics and manufacturing, current digital AI capabilities like language models have minimal direct impact on these manual tasks. The role requires physical presence and cannot be performed via a computer.

occupations.title: Health and Welfare Services Managers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Health and Welfare Services Managers perform a mix of administrative planning and complex interpersonal management. While AI will significantly streamline reporting, resource allocation, and administrative tasks, the core requirements of stakeholder engagement, human management, and nuanced decision-making in the Australian healthcare sector provide a strong buffer against full automation.

occupations.title: Psychologists and Psychotherapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Mental health services in Australia rely heavily on human empathy, trust, and nuanced clinical judgment, which are highly resistant to AI automation. While AI will significantly streamline administrative tasks like clinical note-taking and preliminary assessments, the core therapeutic relationship cannot be automated, limiting overall exposure.

occupations.title: Keyboard Operators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Keyboard Operators perform highly routine, entirely computer-based tasks such as data entry, text processing, and document generation. Current AI technologies, including Optical Character Recognition (OCR), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), and Large Language Models (LLMs), can automate almost all aspects of this work. As Australian businesses rapidly adopt these digital tools to reduce administrative overhead, this occupation faces maximum exposure to AI displacement.

occupations.title: Financial Brokers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Financial broking in Australia, particularly mortgage and insurance broking, is highly computer-based and relies heavily on data analysis, document processing, and product comparison. AI is rapidly automating credit assessments, compliance checks, and market comparisons. While client relationship management and trust remain important human elements, the core technical tasks are highly exposed to AI.

occupations.title: ICT Business and Systems Analysts
occupations.exposure_rationale: ICT Business and Systems Analysts perform predominantly computer-based knowledge work, focusing on documentation, system design, and requirements analysis. Generative AI is highly capable of drafting technical documentation, analyzing system requirements, and generating system plans. While stakeholder management requires human interaction, the core deliverables are highly exposed to AI automation and augmentation.

occupations.title: Automobile Drivers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Automobile driving is a highly physical occupation operating in unpredictable real-world environments, shielding it from current digital AI capabilities like generative text or data analysis. While AI improves GPS routing and dispatch systems, full autonomous vehicle deployment in Australia over the next five years remains limited, keeping direct exposure to digital AI minimal.

occupations.title: Earthmoving Plant Operators
occupations.exposure_rationale: Earthmoving plant operation is a highly physical job requiring real-time spatial awareness and manual control in unpredictable outdoor environments like Australian construction sites and mines. Current digital AI capabilities cannot replace the physical operation of heavy machinery. While some AI-assisted grading or scheduling tools may be used, the core role remains heavily insulated from digital automation over the next five years.

occupations.title: Personal Assistants
occupations.exposure_rationale: Personal Assistants primarily handle scheduling, communication, and organizational tasks that are highly susceptible to automation by AI-driven enterprise tools like Microsoft Copilot. Because the role is almost entirely computer-based and involves processing text and managing digital calendars, AI will significantly boost managerial self-sufficiency, reducing the need for dedicated PAs in Australian corporate and government sectors.

occupations.title: Payroll Clerks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Payroll Clerks perform highly routine, rules-based digital processing tasks that are exceptionally vulnerable to AI automation. While the Australian industrial relations system involving modern awards and superannuation is complex, AI-driven payroll software is increasingly capable of handling these calculations and compliance checks autonomously. Because the role is entirely computer-based and procedural, it faces maximum AI exposure.

occupations.title: Fast Food Cooks
occupations.exposure_rationale: Fast food cooking is an entirely physical role requiring manual dexterity and presence in a commercial kitchen, making it highly resistant to current digital AI. While future robotics may eventually automate food preparation, digital AI tools like LLMs cannot perform the core tasks of assembling and cooking meals, keeping near-term exposure minimal.

occupations.title: Computer Network Professionals
occupations.exposure_rationale: Computer Network Professionals perform highly digital tasks such as network monitoring, configuration, and troubleshooting, which are increasingly automated or augmented by AI tools. While there is some physical hardware management involved, the bulk of the analytical and software-based work is highly exposed to AI-driven efficiencies.

occupations.title: Painting Trades Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Painting is a highly physical trade requiring manual dexterity, spatial awareness, and on-site presence to prepare and finish surfaces. While AI may offer minor assistance to independent contractors for administrative duties like generating quotes or scheduling, the core tasks are entirely immune to current digital AI capabilities.

occupations.title: Social Workers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Social work relies heavily on complex interpersonal interactions, empathy, and navigating unpredictable human situations, which AI cannot replicate. While AI tools will streamline case management, report writing, and administrative tasks, the core requirement for human connection and advocacy keeps overall exposure low.

occupations.title: Private Tutors and Teachers
occupations.exposure_rationale: Teaching physical and performance arts such as dance, drama, and music requires real-time physical feedback, interpersonal connection, and emotional intelligence. While AI can assist with lesson planning, theory, and administrative tasks, the core practical instruction remains highly resistant to automation.

occupations.title: Retail Supervisors
occupations.exposure_rationale: Retail Supervisors operate primarily in physical store environments, focusing on interpersonal tasks like managing staff, handling customer escalations, and overseeing shop floor operations. While AI will increasingly automate back-office administrative tasks such as rostering, inventory forecasting, and sales reporting, the core supervisory and customer-facing duties require human presence and emotional intelligence.

occupations.title: Physiotherapists
occupations.exposure_rationale: Physiotherapy is a highly physical and interpersonal profession requiring hands-on assessment and manual therapy, which digital AI cannot replicate.
